content.claim: Since 2010, Hungarian rural and small-settlement schools increasingly transitioned to faith-based operators (Baptist Aid, Catholic Charities, Greek Catholic, neo-Protestant churches), which enjoy greater student-selection freedom than state schools and are linked to ethnic and socioeconomic segregation patterns.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Attribution of segregation causality: government sources frame as incidental, rights organizations as deliberate structural effect; interpretive dispute unresolved
Correlation between FBO expansion and segregation documented; causal mechanisms (selection, funding advantage, location patterns) not fully disaggregated
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
